hero.paragraph: I shoot motion and the street — the heat off a MotoGP straight, the lean of a classic bike at Montjuïc, the half-second a city gives you on a corner. No staging, no retouching beyond the grade. Just the frame I caught.

body.p1: I came to photography through motorsport — standing at a fence at Montmeló as a teenager, watching bikes disappear into Turn 1 faster than my eyes could follow. I wanted to hold that. A borrowed camera and a cheap telephoto later, I was hooked on the problem: how do you make a still image feel like 300 km/h?
body.p2: The street came after, and for the opposite reason. Where the track is about anticipation and long glass, Barcelona is about being invisible — a 28mm, zone-focused, shooting from the hip, keeping maybe one frame in two hundred. Both disciplines are the same skill wearing different clothes: be early, be honest, press at the right half-second.
body.p3: I don't retouch. The grade is the only intervention — pushing contrast and shadow until the frame matches the light I remember. If it doesn't work straight out of camera, it doesn't work.
body.bodies: Sony a1 · Nikon Z9 · Leica Q2
